talk_title: Overview of the Cancer Research Data Commons infrastructure and usage statistics

speaker.name: Tania Davisson
speaker.affiliation: National Cancer Institute Center for Biomedical Informatics and Information Technology, Chief of Data Ecosystems Branch

MOA_summary: The CRDC provides a unified, cloud-based platform that aggregates genomic, proteomic, imaging, clinical, and other data types across seven specialized data commons, enabling standardized access, harmonized metadata, and scalable analysis without requiring data download.
key_findings: CRDC hosts 533 studies covering over 200,000 subjects and 17 petabytes of data.
Since inception, the cloud resource has performed over 1,600 compute years and offers more than 1,200 public analysis tools.
User engagement includes 100,000 monthly users and 47,000 PubMed citations referencing CRDC data.

talk_title: CCDI Data Ecosystem: resources, enrollment, and data integration

speaker.name: Subhashini Jagu
speaker.affiliation: National Cancer Institute Office of Data Sharing, Branch Chief

clinical_data.population: pediatric and AYA cancer patients on Project Every Child

MOA_summary: CCDI aggregates pediatric cancer clinical, genomic, and imaging data through a unified data model and APIs, enabling cohort building, cross‑resource queries, and rapid molecular characterization (results returned within 21 days) for over 9,000 enrolled patients.
key_findings: CCDI ecosystem currently includes 41 datasets, including Childhood Cancer Survival Study and Pediatric MATCH.
Project Every Child has enrolled >9,000 patients, delivering molecular characterization results within 21 days.
6,000 patients' molecular data from MCI have been released in cBioPortal, with integrated SNV and methylation widgets.
Over 4,000 pathology images are available in the Imaging Data Commons, with AI‑ready processing by DOE.

talk_title: ARPA‑H Biomedical Data Fabric Toolbox integration with CRDC

speaker.name: Erica Kim
speaker.affiliation: National Cancer Institute Center for Biomedical Informatics and IT; ARPA‑H Program Officer

clinical_data.population: real‑world use cases with Netrius tool

MOA_summary: The BDF Toolbox develops AI‑assisted tools (e.g., Netrius DataCord for metadata harmonization, Data Gatherer for literature‑linked dataset extraction, AI chatbots for data exploration) that integrate with CRDC APIs to automate data submission, curation, and discovery, reducing manual effort and improving data readiness for analysis.
key_findings: Netrius DataCord AI‑driven curation platform achieved ≥90% reduction in manual curation time in pilot deployments.
Data Gatherer combines rule‑based and LLM methods to extract dataset metadata from publications, enhancing discoverability.
BioInsight AI chatbot enables plain‑language queries across CRDC genomic, proteomic, and imaging resources.
